samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Chip clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand (a). Input signal representing the multiplier (b). Product output signal. Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation. Shift count register, which counts the number of shifts performed. On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated. if (rst_n == 1'b0) begin <= 16'd0; If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the shift count register (i) is set to 0. if (rst_n == 1'b0) begin <= 16'd0; If the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, the shift count register (i) increments by 1. if (start) begin if ( < 16'd17) begin <= + 16'd1; If the start signal is inactive (!start), the shift count register (i) is reset to 0. if (!start) begin <= 16'd0; Multiplication completion flag generation: On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated. if (rst_n == 1'b0) begin done_r <= 1'b0; If the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, indicating the completion of the multiplication operation, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 1. if (i == 16'd16) begin done_r <= 1'b1; If the shift count register (i) is equal to 17, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is reset to 0. if (i == 16'd17) begin done_r <= 1'b0; Shift and accumulate operation: On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs the shift and accumulate operation. if (rst_n == 1'b0) begin areg <= 16'd0; breg <= 16'd0; yout_r <= 32'd0; end else begin if (start) begin if (i == 16'd0) begin areg <= ain; breg <= bin; end if (i > 16'd0 && i < 16'd17) begin if (areg[i-1] == 1'b1) begin yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1)); end end end end The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r). yout = yout_r;
